title: Stakeholders, Ethics, Accessibility, and Harm Analysis · Stakeholder Case: Ethics Review Route
summary: Primary-user convenience may shift surveillance, labor, exclusion, safety, environmental, or procedural costs onto less visible people. Predict the decision, affected people, evidence strength, failure cost, and transfer limit before seeing a worked case. Planned artifact: Add and verify the capstone ethics review route increment in stakeholder benefit-burden and harm register. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Route domain, legal, accessibility, safety, privacy, or community questions to qualified review and preserve explicit non-claims.
Define prevention, detection, limit, safe failure, appeal, correction, restitution, communication, and retirement for plausible harms.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# capstone-project cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: capstone-project
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Predict Ethics Review Route · Theory Ethics Review Route
steps.instruction:
theory case — Primary-user convenience shifts surveillance, exclusion, labor, safety, environmental, and appeal costs onto people with less product power. The theory variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: defend one bounded product claim

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Ethics Review Route contract for the supplied data.

Evidence target: Predict Ethics Review Route · Theory Ethics Review Route.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for route domain, legal, accessibility, safety, privacy, or community questions to qualified review and preserve explicit non-claims. For Predict Ethics Review Route · Theory Ethics Review Route, keep Ethics Review Route connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: Primary-user convenience shifts surveillance, exclusion, labor, safety, environmental, and appeal costs onto people with less product power. content.title: Commit before feedback
content.text: Choose from the stated data before opening the worked model. Record which Ethics Review Route condition decided your prediction.

content.label: Authority to consult after committing
content.value: ACM Code of Ethics and Professional Conduct — Controls public-good reasoning, harm avoidance, honesty, fairness, privacy, security, competence, review, and accountability.

hints: For Predict Ethics Review Route · Theory Ethics Review Route, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Ethics Review Route precondition, or invariant that must hold.

options.text: A software team can resolve every domain risk through technical controls. That claim fails the predict ethics review route evidence boundary for Ethics Review Route.

For predict ethics review route, apply Ethics Review Route without its stated preconditions, omit the stakeholder benefit-burden and harm register trace and counterexample, and treat one plausible output as complete proof. This does not satisfy Ethics Review Route for stakeholder benefit-burden and harm register.
Route domain, legal, accessibility, safety, privacy, or community questions to qualified review and preserve explicit non-claims. Apply it to the concrete input and state the observable result.

content.text: Route domain, legal, accessibility, safety, privacy, or community questions to qualified review and preserve explicit non-claims. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name the stakeholder decision, affected people, authority, assumption, revision, artifact, observable behavior, changed case, rejected failure, accessibility, security, privacy, operation, recovery, contribution, residual risk, and transfer limit; then calibrate the public claim to that exact evidence. Apply that move specifically to Ethics Review Route while producing stakeholder benefit-burden and harm register.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Primary-user convenience shifts surveillance, exclusion, labor, safety, environmental, and appeal costs onto people with less product power. The worked example variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Route domain, legal, accessibility, safety, privacy, or community questions to qualified review and preserve explicit non-claims. A defensible solution applies Ethics Review Route, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to stakeholder benefit-burden and harm register.

content.code:
# Worked focus: capstone-ethics-review-route — Route domain, legal, accessibility, safety, privacy, or community questions to qualified review and preserve explicit non-claims.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: changed-product-3
focus: capstone-ethics-review-route
decision-evidence: Route domain, legal, accessibility, safety, privacy, or community questions to qualified review and preserve explicit non-claims.
stakeholder: affected-user review panel
decision: continue only if the changed task meets its outcome and guardrails
artifact: revision-bound evidence packet
claim: one bounded behavior survives the changed context
verify: reproduce the task, inspect the artifact, and compare the predeclared measure
changed-case: different access need, input, constraint, and dependency failure
failure: reject plausible output without causal, human, recovery, or ownership evidence
repair: isolate the first unsupported transition, correct it, and add a regression
owner: named learner and stakeholder reviewer
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research and release environment
content.caption: Runnable transformation example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

steps.title: Defend the Ethics Review Route model · Theory Ethics Review Route
